matches.claim_sentence: In 2025, contributions reported by registered federal lobbyists concentrated on the members of Congress who decide whether legislation moves.
Rep. Brett Guthrie of Kentucky was the top recipient, with $694,295 from 177 distinct lobbying registrants.
Guthrie chairs the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

matches.claim_sentence: Rep. Jason Smith of Missouri, who chairs Ways and Means, received $674,775 from lobbyist filers.
Sen. Mark Warner of Virginia received $632,009.
For the money-committee chairs, lobbyist contributions amounted to roughly 17 percent of Guthrie's entire campaign haul for the 2026 cycle.

The largest 2025 lobbying client by reported income is fictional: a Delaware filer calling itself the State of Loc Nation claimed $20,000,000 of lobbying income per quarter, $80 million for the year after amendment-aware de-duplication.

matches.claim_sentence: Tencent's reported lobbying income reached $4.04 million in 2025, roughly five times its level from a year earlier.
The Department of Defense added Tencent to its list of Chinese military companies in January 2025.
